chapter_title.ar: بَابُ مَا يَلْبَسُ الْمُحْرِمُ مِنَ الثِّيَابِ وَ مَا يُكْرَهُ لَهُ لِبَاسُهُ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 83 – What the one in Ihraam can wear, from the clothes, and what is disliked for him, his clothing

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (7/283)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (7/283)
text: سُئِلَ عَنْ خَلُوقِ الْكَعْبَةِ لِلْمُحْرِمِ أَ يُغْسَلُ مِنْهُ الثَّوْبُ قَالَ لَا هُوَ طَهُورٌ ثُمَّ قَالَ إِنَّ بِثَوْبِي مِنْهُ لَطْخاً .

en.hubeali: Ahmad, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from one of our companions,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullahasws, said, ‘Heasws was asked about the perfumes of the Kabah for the one in Ihraam, should he wash the clothes from it?’ Heasws said: ‘No, it is clean’. Then heasws said: ‘Myasws clothes have been stained from it’.
en.sarwar: 15. Ahmad has narrated from ibn Faddal from af-Mufaddaf ibn Safih from Layth af-Muradiy who has said the foiiowing: “I once asked abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about printed cloths; if a man can use it for Ihram. He (the Imam) said, ‘Yes, only al-Mulahham (mixed with silk) kind of cloth is detestable.’”
